utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan: 1) health_coverage: Present with costs (monthly and annual) and description. Fully specified per format. 2) property_coverage: Present with home/contents insurance with costs? The plan includes home_office_protection and technology_protection, each with monthly/annual costs and limits. This satisfies home/contents insurance with costs. 3) travel_coverage: Present, but does it include travel insurance with costs? It states 'Included in comprehensive package, no additional cost' with dates. Cost is specified as no additional cost; acceptable as a cost specification. 4) additional_services: Present with items and costs; fits 'any enhancement services with costs'. 5) total_budget: Present with monthly/annual totals and breakdown. All 5 items are included and have the required cost details. Therefore, 5/5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: I matched each named item in the final coverage plan to the closest exact name in the ground-truth ratings. Only items explicitly listed in the ground-truth can receive ratings. Matches found: SecureLife Basic Plan (health) -> rating 4 under primary_health_coverage.physical_health; Home Office Protection -> rating 7 under property_enhancement_services; Technology Protection -> rating 8 under property_enhancement_services. The travel coverage in the final plan is generic with no provider name, so no match. Additional services such as professional liability, entertainment system discount, and gaming equipment coverage: Entertainment System Discount maps to property_enhancement_services 'Entertainment System Discount' (rating 3); Gaming Equipment Coverage maps to property_enhancement_services 'Gaming Equipment Coverage' (rating 1). Professional liability is not present in ground-truth, so no rating.
